statement: For a finite-dimensional space, the natural map to the double dual is an isomorphism.
lean_statement:
theorem nonempty_linearEquiv_doubleDual_of_finiteDimensional
    {K V : Type*} [Field K] [AddCommGroup V] [Module K V] [FiniteDimensional K V] :
    Nonempty (V ≃ₗ[K] Module.Dual K (Module.Dual K V))
proof:
Proof: choose a basis of `V`, so `V ≃ K^n`. For `K^n` the evaluation map into the double dual is an
isomorphism (a linear functional is determined by its values on the standard basis), and the
statement is preserved by linear equivalence. Transporting this isomorphism back along the chosen
basis gives a linear equivalence `V ≃ₗ[K] Module.Dual K (Module.Dual K V)`.
